title: Evangelion Platinum thin pack collection 90
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Platinum thin pack collection $90?!?!             Quote Post by Ataru » Thu Aug 18, 2005 6:46 pm animeondvd lists this as being in a diamond preview: Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Collection Thinkpak - $89.98 just last month I spent more than that on 5 of the 7 discs. how are they going to handle the inserts for a thinpack set? Perhaps that will be the silverlining for those of us with the first edtion of platinum?

[]: Re: Evangelion Platinum THINPACK $90?!?!             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Aug 18, 2005 6:47 pm Ataru wrote: animeondvd lists this as being in a diamond preview: Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Collection Thinkpak - $89.98 just last month I spent more than that on 5 of the 7 discs. how are they going to handle the inserts for a thinpack set? Perhaps that will be the silverlining for those of us with the first edtion of platinum?

[]:             Quote Post by Ataru » Thu Aug 18, 2005 6:50 pm yes they do suck. but $90 list vs. $210 list = ouch.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Aug 18, 2005 7:37 pm Thinpacks suck, don't they? Well, compared to the real box-sets. I admit to having a few, but I don't love them.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Thu Aug 18, 2005 7:42 pm Well I only paid $US 20 bucks each ($US 25 for vol. one with the box), for a total of $US 145, which is fine, I guess, but the thinpack better be the shitiest thing ever, cause i dont' wanna regret getting it. I would almost agree with Hexon, that it should be of the same size as my X-files, but I'm stilled POed about the thinpack buyers getting sucha discount...><

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Fri Aug 19, 2005 12:18 pm Any images of this set out there? Thin pack as in the rail plastic cases or as in (the matrix gift set) cases?

[]: Thinpack             Quote Post by Enixon » Fri Aug 19, 2005 6:11 pm Forgive me if I sound a bit n00bish, but, what is a thinpack?

[]: Re: Thinpack             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Aug 19, 2005 6:20 pm Enixon wrote: Forgive me if I sound a bit n00bish, but, what is a thinpack?

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Fri Aug 19, 2005 7:00 pm [url]http://www.sleevetown.com/dvd-case.shtml[/url] (Scroll down to THINpak.) A great space saver which can look very stylish if designed correctly - I might purchase this set... shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Aug 19, 2005 8:52 pm Deciding to rerelease titles as thinpacks is, IMHO, a rare example of ADV doing something really right. This is why churchy people are always stressing the importance of longsuffering. I only have the first three volumes. Purchasing this will be way cheaper than completing the set.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Sat Aug 20, 2005 1:18 am Hexon.Arq wrote: Thinpacks suck, don't they? Well, compared to the real box-sets. I admit to having a few, but I don't love them.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 22, 2005 12:29 am Like what? Liner notes? That's what Eva Monkey is for.

[]:             Quote Post by blind51de » Mon Aug 22, 2005 6:01 am Maybe, but Rahxephon/Noir/FMP are still lost.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 22, 2005 5:42 pm I look at it this way: if I were living in Japan, watching this shit on TV, what are the odds they'd send me the liner notes? Love is all you need...er, I mean DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Aug 26, 2005 3:29 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: I look at it this way: if I were living in Japan, watching this shit on TV, what are the odds they'd send me the liner notes? Love is all you need...er, I mean DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Tue Aug 30, 2005 12:22 pm I always break the stupid thinpacks by stepping on them >_> The thick, annoying DVDs can withstand my confusing struggles to get out of my room to go pee in the middle of the night. I'm...very messy...

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Aug 30, 2005 3:14 pm Don't be.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Tue Aug 30, 2005 4:14 pm I like it big and solid. It looks good! In my opinion, that is...

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Aug 30, 2005 5:00 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Don't be.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Wed Aug 31, 2005 12:36 am sachiel wrote: Hexon.Arq wrote: Don't be.
